title: silene vulgaris var. marginata
intro: Silene vulgaris var. marginata, also known as Bladder Campion, is a perennial plant species in the carnation family, characterized by its pink or white flowers with distinctive white markings. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Bladder Campion include its ability to soothe irritated throats and reduce mucus production, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
